title: Rectangular frame
abstract: The invention relates to a rectangular frame that comprises two parallel positioned framework beams, two transverse beams, and four corners, in which the extremities of the framework beams and the extremities of the transverse beams are resiliently and by means of ball joints connected to a corner in each of the four corners of the rectangular frame. The framework beams, the transverse beams, and/or the corners comprise compartments that can be filled with gas and/or water in order to be able to float the rectangular frame or to submerse the latter into a submersed state.
claims:
Claims Claims 1 Rectangular frame, comprising two parallel positioned framework beams, two transverse beams, and four corners, in which the extremities of the framework beams and the extremities of the transverse beams are resiliently and by means of ball joints connected to a corner in each of the four corners of the rectangular frame, in which the framework beams, the transverse beams, and/or the comers comprise compartments that can be filled with gas and/or water in order to be able to float the rectangular frame or to submerse the latter into a submersed state. 2. Rectangular frame according to claim 1 , in which the compartments are connected with closing means to a reservoir that contains a pressurised gas. 3. Rectangular frame according to one of the claims 1-2, in which the corners of the rectangular frame comprise means to anchor the rectangular frame to the ground. 4. Rectangular frame according to claim 3, in which the means to anchor the rectangular frame to the ground comprise an anchor that is situated at the bottom side of a shaft, whereby the shaft is vertically movable and positioned in an opening in the corner, and whereby part of the shaft extends above the corner, and part of it extends below the corner, and whereby the top end of the part of the shaft that extends above the comer is connected to a comer by means of one or more actuators. 5. Rectangular frame according to claim 4, in which the anchor is a screw anchor or a suction anchor. 6. Rectangular frame according to claim 5, in which the anchor is a suction anchor that is connected by means of a ball joint. 7. Rectangular frame according to claim 6, in which the bottom side of the suction anchor comprises a rotatable disc with teeth. 8. Rectangular frame according to claim 7, in which the anchor is a screw anchor that comprises a hollow axis through which a helical cutting blade is positioned, and in 

 which in the wall of the hollow axis and the helical cutting blade outflow openings are foreseen that are connected to a supply tube in the hollow axis for a gas or a liquid. 9. Rectangular frame according to one of the claims 1-8, in which the comers of the rectangular frame comprise a supporting means. 10. Rectangular frame according to claim 9, in which the supporting means are resiliently connected to the corners, and in which the supporting means are connected to the corners by means of linear actuators that are adjustable in the vertical direction. 11. Rectangular frame according to one of the claims 9-10, in which the supporting means are a sled, a wheel, or a caterpillar track. 12. Rectangular frame according to one of the claims 1-11 , comprising one or more thrusters with which a vertical and/or horizontal displacement of the rectangular frame in a submersible state can be carried out. 13. Rectangular frame according to one of the claims 1-12, in which the frame furthermore comprises a movable bridge that at both of its extremities is movably connected to the two framework beams, in such a way that a displacement of the bridge in the direction of both transversal beams is possible. 14. Rectangular frame according to claim 13, in which the movable bridge is connected to the transverse beams by means of winch cables with which the bridge can be moved. 15. Rectangular frame according to one of the claims 13-14, in which the movable bridge at both extremities comprises a guiding shaft, whereby through the opening of each of the shafts passes one of the two parallel positioned framework beams, in such a way that the movable bridge can move in the longitudinal direction of the framework beams. 16. Rectangular frame according to claim 15, in which the guiding shafts at the inside comprise resilient wheelsets.
